verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a multi-pass lysosomal ABC half-transporter that resides exclusively in the limiting membrane of lysosomes. Its N-terminal TMD0 encodes a dominant lysosomal targeting signal and interacts with LAMP-1/LAMP-2B. A 2019 RUSH-assay study explicitly demonstrated 'TAPL is not found on the plasma membrane.' No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program, ectodomain shedding biology, or documented PM minority pool exists. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no gene-specific evidence of cancer/apoptosis/activation-driven outer-leaflet display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — high testis/DC expression is transcriptional, protein remains lysosomal; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no published evidence for ABCB9 reaching the PM via lysosomal exocytosis; (4) dual_localization — PM residence of the truncated coreTAPL construct is an artificial experimental artifact, not native biology; literature explicitly rules out PM pool for full-length protein; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no secreted form; not applicable to a TM lysosomal protein.
